title_en: Hadiths 21–30: Purification of the Soul and Ethical Principles
title_ar: Hadiths 21–30: Purification of the Soul and Ethical Principles
content_en:
The third group of hadiths in al-Arba'een deepens the exploration of personal ethics, the purification of the soul, and the comprehensive moral framework that Islam provides for human conduct. These hadiths are among those most frequently cited by scholars of Islamic spirituality and character development.

Hadith 21 — 'Say: I believe in Allah, then be steadfast' — is a remarkable compression of the entire Islamic moral project: declare your faith and then live consistently with it, without vacillation or compromise. Ibn Rajab's commentary on this hadith traces the concept of istiqamah (steadfastness) through the Quranic references and the sayings of the Companions, arguing that Umar ibn al-Khattab identified it as the most comprehensive single principle of the religion.

Hadith 22 on the prohibition of squandering property and the obligation to preserve it, and Hadith 23 — 'Purification is half of faith; saying Alhamdulillah fills the scale; SubhanAllah and Alhamdulillah fill what is between the heavens and the earth; prayer is a light; charity is a proof; patience is an illumination; and the Quran is a proof for you or against you' — are both foundational statements that Ibn Rajab treats as summaries of the experiential dimension of Islam.

Hadith 25 — 'Charity is due upon every joint of a person every day the sun rises: judging justly between two people is charity; assisting a man with his mount is charity; a good word is charity; every step you take towards the prayer is charity; and removing a harmful thing from the road is charity' — expands the understanding of sadaqah beyond financial giving to encompass the full range of beneficial action in daily life. Hadiths 27 and 29 on righteousness and the avoidance of wrongdoing establish the comprehensive Quranic principle: 'Righteousness is good character, and wrongdoing is what pricks your conscience and you hate for others to know about.'
content_id:
Kelompok ketiga hadits dalam al-Arba'een memperdalam eksplorasi etika pribadi, penyucian jiwa, dan kerangka moral komprehensif yang Islam berikan bagi perilaku manusia. Hadits-hadits ini termasuk yang paling sering dikutip oleh para ulama spiritualitas Islam dan pengembangan akhlak.

Hadits 21 — 'Katakanlah: Aku beriman kepada Allah, kemudian istiqamahlah' — merupakan ringkasan luar biasa dari seluruh proyek moral Islam: nyatakan keimananmu lalu jalani hidup secara konsisten dengannya, tanpa kebimbangan atau kompromi. Komentar Ibnu Rajab terhadap hadits ini menelusuri konsep istiqamah melalui referensi-referensi Al-Qur'an dan perkataan para Sahabat, dengan berargumen bahwa Umar ibn al-Khattab mengidentifikasinya sebagai prinsip tunggal paling komprehensif dalam agama ini.

Hadits 22 tentang larangan menyia-nyiakan harta dan kewajiban menjaganya, serta Hadits 23 — 'Bersuci adalah separuh iman; mengucapkan Alhamdulillah memenuhi timbangan; SubhanAllah dan Alhamdulillah memenuhi apa yang ada di antara langit dan bumi; shalat adalah cahaya; sedekah adalah bukti; sabar adalah penerang; dan Al-Qur'an adalah hujjah bagimu atau atasmu' — keduanya merupakan pernyataan mendasar yang diperlakukan oleh Ibnu Rajab sebagai ringkasan dimensi pengamalan Islam.

Hadits 25 — 'Setiap ruas tulang seseorang wajib bersedekah setiap hari ketika matahari terbit: berlaku adil di antara dua orang adalah sedekah; membantu seseorang menaikkan kendaraannya adalah sedekah; ucapan yang baik adalah sedekah; setiap langkah yang kamu ayunkan menuju shalat adalah sedekah; dan menyingkirkan sesuatu yang membahayakan dari jalan adalah sedekah' — memperluas pemahaman tentang sedekah melampaui pemberian harta untuk mencakup seluruh rangkaian tindakan bermanfaat dalam kehidupan sehari-hari. Hadits 27 dan 29 tentang kebajikan dan menghindari perbuatan buruk menegaskan prinsip Al-Qur'an yang komprehensif: 'Kebajikan adalah akhlak yang baik, dan perbuatan buruk adalah sesuatu yang mengganjal hatimu dan kamu tidak suka jika orang lain mengetahuinya.'
title_id: Hadits 21–30: Penyucian Jiwa dan Prinsip-Prinsip Etika
content_ar:
The third group of hadiths in al-Arba'een deepens the exploration of personal ethics, the purification of the soul, and the comprehensive moral framework that Islam provides for human conduct. These hadiths are among those most frequently cited by scholars of Islamic spirituality and character development.

Hadith 21 — 'Say: I believe in الله, then be steadfast' — is a remarkable compression of the entire Islamic moral project: declare your faith and then live consistently with it, without vacillation or compromise. Ibn Rajab's commentary on this hadith traces the concept of istiqamah (steadfastness) through the Quranic references and the sayings of the Companions, arguing that Umar ibn al-Khattab identified it as the most comprehensive single principle of the religion.

Hadith 22 on the prohibition of squandering property and the obligation to preserve it, and Hadith 23 — 'Purification is half of faith; saying Alhamdulillah fills the scale; Subhanالله and Alhamdulillah fill what is between the heavens and the earth; prayer is a light; charity is a proof; patience is an illumination; and the Quran is a proof for you or against you' — are both foundational statements that Ibn Rajab treats as summaries of the experiential dimension of Islam.

Hadith 25 — 'Charity is due upon every joint of a person every day the sun rises: judging justly between two people is charity; assisting a man with his mount is charity; a good word is charity; every step you take towards the prayer is charity; and removing a harmful thing from the road is charity' — expands the understanding of sadaqah beyond financial giving to encompass the full range of beneficial action in daily life. Hadiths 27 and 29 on righteousness and the avoidance of wrongdoing establish the comprehensive Quranic principle: 'Righteousness is good character, and wrongdoing is what pricks your conscience and you hate for others to know about.'
